verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is synthesized as a large latent complex (LLC): the mature TGF-β1 dimer remains non-covalently associated with its LAP (Latency Associated Peptide) prodomain, which in turn is disulfide-bonded to LTBP proteins (LTBP1/3) that are covalently crosslinked to ECM. However, the LLC can also be tethered to the cell surface via covalent disulfide bonds between LAP and transmembrane GARP (LRRC32) or LRRC33 on regulatory T cells, platelets, and myeloid cells — forming a wash-resistant, surface-displayed latent complex. This is a documented stable_surface_attachment via TM scaffold partners GARP/LRRC33. The tethered surface form is biologically important for integrin-mediated activation and is targeted therapeutically (e.g., anti-GARP-TGFβ1 antibodies in clinical trials). The dominant secreted/ECM-deposited pool is not itself surface-anchored on most cells, hence contextual rather than yes.
